title: MockAgents Agent Definition
description: Declarative configuration for a MockAgents mock agent.

name.description: Unique agent name. Must be lowercase kebab-case.

AgentSpec.description: Agent specification including protocol, tools, and behavior.

model.description: Model name reported in API responses. Defaults to 'mock-agent'.

systemPrompt.description: System prompt included in responses where the protocol supports it.

ToolDefinition.description: A tool the agent can invoke.

parameters.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters.

validate.description: Validate scenario-emitted call arguments against `parameters`; violations resolve to an INVALID_PARAMETERS error result.

error_rate.description: Probability (0-1) that a call to this tool resolves to an injected error result.

ToolResponseRule.description: Maps a match condition to a tool response or error.

match.description: Parameter values to match against. Omit for default response.

default.description: If true, this rule is the fallback when no other match succeeds.

scenarios.description: Response scenarios. At least one is required.

StrictToolsConfig.description: Strict-tools knob: opt-in request-side strictness mirroring real-API behavior (round-trip tool ids, tool_choice forcing, parallel caps, strict:true schemas). Fleet default via MOCKAGENTS_STRICT_TOOLS.

level.description: off = lenient (default); warn = run checks, log + X-Mockagents-Strict-Violation header; strict = fail with the provider's real 400. A block without a level implies strict.

ids.description: Round-trip tool id validation: tool results must reference echoed call ids, and every call must be answered.

tool_choice.description: Enforce required/named tool_choice (forced-call synthesis) and the parallel-call cap.

schemas.description: Validate strict:true function schemas against the structured-outputs subset.

MatchRule.description: Conditions under which a scenario activates.

content_contains.description: Substring to match in user message.

content_regex.description: Regex to match in user message.

has_image.description: Match only when the latest user turn carries at least one image part (true) or none (false).

ScenarioResponse.description: Response produced when a scenario matches. Provide at least one of content, refusal, or tool_calls.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls to simulate in the response.

finish_reason.description: Override the emitted finish/stop reason (OpenAI-style, e.g. 'length' for a truncated response, 'content_filter'). Mapped per provider (FB-03).

refusal.description: Emit an assistant refusal instead of content — OpenAI's structured message.refusal field (refusal text as content on Anthropic/Gemini) (FB-03).

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata to include in the response.

HallucinationSpec.description: Marks this response as a deterministic hallucination fixture (FB-02). The content is the planted bad output; the mock advertises it via the X-Mockagents-Hallucination response header.

ToolCallSpec.description: A tool call the agent simulates.

name.description: Name of the tool to call.

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the tool.

raw_arguments.description: Emit this string VERBATIM as the tool call's arguments (e.g. malformed JSON like '{"city":') instead of marshaling arguments — to test a client's tool-call argument parser. OpenAI only (FB-03).

enabled.description: Whether streaming is enabled. Default: false.

chunk_delay_ms.description: Delay between chunks in milliseconds. Default: 50.

ttft_ms.description: Time-to-first-token: delay before the first content chunk.

tokens_per_sec.description: When > 0, pace content chunks at this rate (overrides chunk_delay_ms).

jitter_ms.description: Deterministic +/- jitter (ms) added to each inter-chunk delay.

ttft_p50_ms.description: Median time-to-first-token (ms). With ttft_p95_ms, TTFT is sampled from a lognormal fit to the two percentiles, overriding ttft_ms (FB-05).

itl_p50_ms.description: Median inter-token latency (ms, per token). With itl_p95_ms, the per-chunk delay is sampled from a lognormal and scaled by token count, overriding tokens_per_sec (FB-05).

itl_p95_ms.description: 95th-percentile inter-token latency (ms, per token). Pair with itl_p50_ms.

truncate_after_chunks.description: When > 0, end the stream after N content chunks with no finish frame / [DONE] (truncated stream fault).

malformed.description: Emit one invalid-JSON SSE frame at the stop point, then end the stream (malformed-chunk fault).

ChaosConfig.description: Fault injection settings. All fields optional.

enabled.description: Explicitly toggle chaos on/off. Defaults to true when any sub-section is present.

preset.description: Named shorthand that expands into the concrete chaos sub-sections at load time. Explicit sub-sections override the preset.

distribution.description: Latency distribution. Defaults to uniform when min/max differ, else fixed.

rate.description: Probability of injecting an error (0.0 to 1.0).

status_code.description: HTTP status code for injected errors.

status_codes.description: When set, a status code is picked uniformly at random from this list.

timeout.description: Force injected failures to simulate a timeout.

timeout_ms.description: How long to sleep before returning the synthetic timeout.

message.description: Human-readable error message surfaced to clients.

fail_first.description: Stateful trigger: deterministically fail the first N requests to this agent, then recover (every later request succeeds). Takes precedence over rate. For testing client retry/backoff.

connection.description: Connection-LAYER fault injected before any HTTP response (FB-03 slice 5). The server hijacks the TCP connection; on HTTP/2 it falls back to a 502.

mode.description: reset/peer-reset: TCP RST; empty: close with no bytes; random/random-then-close/garbage: write garbage bytes then close.

rate.description: Probability of injecting the fault per request (0.0 to 1.0).

fail_first.description: Stateful trigger: fault the first N requests to this agent, then recover. Takes precedence over rate.
